title: Cognitive Operator Standard
purpose: Define reusable thinking operators as activation-grade substrate with evidence, dogfood receipts, validation, and navigation/task-selection hooks.

navigation_use.claim: Compliance row proves registry and dogfood validator coverage for cognitive-operator substrate; it is not mutation authority.

activation_contract.rule: A cognitive operator is usable only when its activation block names concrete trigger phrases, opens_when conditions, and skip_when constraints.
activation_contract.anti_pattern: Treating an operator as inspirational prose without a bounded activation test.

dogfood_contract.rule: Every active operator must have at least one receipt proving it changed a live task decision, artifact, route, or validation result.

counterevidence_extension.purpose: Operators that choose, install, or defend a thinking mode may require explicit negative evidence so future agents can see why the selected claim survived.

causal_trial_extension.purpose: Operators that claim cognition changed must retain a pre/post causal comparison instead of only retrospective rationale.

composition_extension.purpose: Operators that coordinate multiple cognitive operators must retain the ordered chain, handoffs, and sequence result instead of leaving composition in prose.

affordance_passport_extension.purpose: Operators that should be selected by future agents must carry row-owned affordance fields instead of relying only on hardcoded prompt phrases or prose activation blocks.

passport_propagation_extension.purpose: Operators that migrate trigger-only cognitive operators into passport-bearing rows must retain the source row, before/after coverage, remaining rows, and the rule for future propagation.

route_lease_extension.purpose: Operators that consume entry/context route leases must retain the lease id, selected lane, direct-action boundary, forbidden follow-up routes, and validation condition so navigation becomes action instead of repeated routing.

prompt_route_assimilation_extension.purpose: Operators that convert prompt-derived route misses into retained future routing affordances must retain the source lens, phrase evidence, target surface, mutation, validation prompt, and retention check.
prompt_route_assimilation_extension.retention_policy: Private or high-context prompt wording must be abstracted into the smallest reusable route phrase before it is retained; do not copy prompt lore into broad aliases when a typed operator, concept, standard, or option surface can carry the route.

pressure_reduction_extension.purpose: Operators that reduce live backlog, queue, receipt, or ledger pressure into one bounded action must retain the competing pressure surfaces, decision axes, selected pressure, rejected pressures, status-binding target, and validation return condition.
pressure_reduction_extension.trace_source: ./repo-python kernel.py --option-surface task_ledger --band cluster_flag; ./repo-python kernel.py --option-surface row_patches --band cluster_flag; ./repo-python kernel.py --option-surface transform_job_receipts --band cluster_flag

operator_accretion_extension.purpose: Operators that decide whether to add, merge, extend, cap, or reject proposed cognitive operators must retain the candidate, nearest existing operators, novelty test, merge/extend decision, accretion risk, install-or-cap decision, and future reentry rule.
operator_accretion_extension.trace_source: ./repo-python kernel.py --context-pack "decide whether this autonomous seed should add another cognitive operator or merge reuse an existing operator before installing more" --context-budget 12000; ./repo-python kernel.py --option-surface cognitive_operators --band flag

attention_frame_extension.purpose: Operators that preserve task-local attention must retain the frame surface, binding, handles, freshness constraints, mutation boundary, rehydration result, and validation return condition instead of relying on chat memory or broad rereads.
attention_frame_extension.trace_source: ./repo-python kernel.py --attention-state latest --band flag; ./repo-python kernel.py --session-diagnostics --lens all --last 30 --store both --json

landing_handoff_extension.purpose: Operators that preserve validated-but-unlanded work must retain the blocker, owned paths, validation evidence, authority requirement, handoff packet, status binding, and reentry rule instead of leaving the next agent to rediscover a stranded slice.

integration_contract.navigation: The operator must be browsable through `./repo-python kernel.py --option-surface cognitive_operators --band flag` and a card-band row.
integration_contract.memory: The registry row persists the operator; dogfood receipts persist use evidence.
integration_contract.validation: The validator must reject active operators without evidence, activation, integration, and dogfood receipts.
integration_contract.task_selection: The row must name task_selection_hooks so future agents can decide whether to run the operator before picking ordinary backlog work.
integration_contract.future_seed_behavior: The row must include future_seed_behavior that tells cold agents how the operator affects first-pass reasoning.

safety_rules: Cognitive operator rows are not raw seed and must not claim operator voice authority.
A dogfood receipt is evidence of use, not proof of permanent doctrine correctness.
Operators may route Task Ledger captures, but they must not silently create high-blast work.
If an operator cannot be installed in the current turn, create a cap with failure evidence, acceptance criteria, first implementation surface, and a proposed experiment.
